paper_title: Heavy Metal Pollution in Lake Sediments in China

methods.description: Systematic collection of studies related to heavy metal pollution from lake sediments in China over the past 10 years, focusing on eight priority heavy metals as designated by the USEPA.

steps.description: We systematically collected studies related to heavy metal pollution from lake sediments in China from the past 10 years.
steps.searchable_summary: collect studies on heavy metal pollution in Chinese lake sediments from the past decade

steps.description: The data from 110 lakes located in 24 provinces throughout China were collected and analyzed, from the main literature databases including Web of Science and China Knowledge Full-text Literature Database.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch data on heavy metal content from literature databases

methods.description: Sampling strategies and processing methods for lake sediment samples, including collection, drying, sieving, pulverizing, digestion, and heavy metal concentration determination.

steps.description: The lake sediment samples were mainly collected to a depth of 0-5 cm, 0-10 cm, 0-20 cm and then mixed thoroughly to give a composite sample.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and mix lake sediment samples to specified depths

steps.description: In general, they were air-dried at room temperature and then sieved and pulverized.
steps.searchable_summary: air-dry, sieve, and pulverize sediment samples

steps.description: The sieved lake sediment samples were completely digested with a mixed acid such as HF, HCLO4, HNO3, HCL, Aqua regia or H2SO4.
steps.searchable_summary: digest sediment samples with mixed acids

steps.description: Finally, the total concentrations of eight heavy metals were determined by ICP, ICP-AES, ICP-OES, ICP-MS, AAS, AFS, XFS, XRF, or CVAFS.
steps.searchable_summary: determine heavy metal concentrations using various analytical techniques

methods.description: Statistical analysis and visualization of heavy metal concentration data, including comparisons with environmental standards and percentile calculations.

steps.description: In order to corroborate the comparisons with standards, percentile values (10th, 25th, 50th, 75th, and 90th), grades I and II environment quality standard for soils in China, background values for soils in China, and mean concentration of the heavy metal are presented.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentile values and compare with environmental standards

steps.description: The distribution map of the lakes included in this analysis is presented in Figure 1.
All the calculations, the charts, and statistical analyses for the data were performed with EXCEL 2003 and ORIGIN 8.5.
steps.searchable_summary: perform calculations and create charts using EXCEL and ORIGIN
